title: Dolichos ghaticus
taxonomy: Dolichos ghaticus is classified under the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. It is a type of flowering plant, and its classification also includes the subfamily Faboideae. The genus Dolichos is part of the tribe Phaseoleae. This classification indicates that Dolichos ghaticus is a member of the larger group of leguminous plants. Further classification may include the species level, but the exact classification of Dolichos ghaticus may require more specific information.
uses: Dolichos ghaticus is a plant species used in traditional medicine, primarily in India. The plant's extracts are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are utilized to treat various health conditions. Dolichos ghaticus is also used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its purported antidiarrheal properties. The plant's roots and seeds are used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat conditions like fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Further research is required to fully understand the medicinal potential of Dolichos ghaticus.
benefits: Dolichos ghaticus, also known as Indian bean or horse gram, has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its health benefits. It is rich in protein, fiber, and minerals, making it a nutritious addition to a healthy diet. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including lowering cholesterol levels, regulating blood sugar, and aiding in weight management. Additionally, it is believed to have antioxidant properties that help protect against cell damage and inflammation. Consuming Dolichos ghaticus is thought to promote overall well-being and reduce the risk of chronic diseases.

side_effects: Dolichos ghaticus, also known as Horse Gram, is a legume commonly used in traditional medicine, but its side effects are not well-documented. However, consumption of Horse Gram in excess may cause gastrointestinal upset, diarrhea, and nausea due to its high fiber and protein content. Additionally, individuals with kidney problems or those taking certain medications should be cautious when consuming Horse Gram, as it may exacerbate these conditions. The high oxalate content in Horse Gram may also cause kidney stones or worsen existing kidney issues in susceptible individuals. More research is needed to fully understand the potential side effects of consuming Dolichos ghaticus.
